feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: A simple, whitespace theme for academics. Based on [*folio](https://github.com/bogoli/-folio) design.

entry.title: My Team Won the 2025 Huawei Tech Arena on LLM Inference Optimization

link.title: My Team Won the 2025 Huawei Tech Arena on LLM Inference Optimization

entry.content:
Dublin, Monday 24th November – Oluwadara Adedeji, a student at UCD School of Computer Science, has secured first place at the Huawei Tech Arena 2025, partnering with three students from Dublin City University to solve one of the most pressing challenges in artificial intelligence development.

Team Dynamo A - composed of Adedeji (UCD), Chinonso Osuji (DCU), Jidenna Okwarah (DCU), and Christopher Mc Aleer (DCU)—competed against over 300 enrolled students from universities across Ireland in the hackathon’s ninth edition, ultimately claiming the top prize of €5,000….read more

entry.title: How I Passed the AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty Exam To Earn My First AWS Certification

link.title: How I Passed the AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty Exam To Earn My First AWS Certification

entry.content: I recently passed the AWS Machine Learning Specialty exam with a score of ~880/1000 (750 minimum to pass) after two months of preparation. While this isn’t my first cloud certification (I also hold an Azure Fundamentals certification), it’s my first AWS certification. Known as one of the hardest and trickiest AWS exams, this milestone is the culmination of my machine learning journey with AWS services. In this post, I’ll share why I pursued this certification, how I prepared, what I learned, and how you can tailor your own preparation strategy….read more

entry.title: Genius Plays, AI Displays, and Stallion Parades - My Summer School Experience at ML Labs

link.title: Genius Plays, AI Displays, and Stallion Parades - My Summer School Experience at ML Labs

entry.content: It has been some weeks since my first ML Labs Summer School, and I continue to reminisce about the knowledge and insights I gained from this exciting and instructive program. One of my favourite experiences was getting advice on navigating the PhD from graduates and industry partners. I was also able to relearn the fundamental concepts in different areas of AI, especially Gen AI…read more

entry.title: Twitter feed
entry.content:
Follow me on Twitter

Tweet

Machine Learning Monitoring

During the machine learning cycle, machine learning models can degrade due to issues such as data or concept drift. It is for this reason that it is crucial to monitor machine learning models and data at inference. I am happy to share the monitoring aspect of my MLOps project.

— Dara! (@darasiemi) July 23, 2024

Timeline

Tweets by darasiemi

entry.title: Ideate to Innovate
entry.content:
Who is the greatest inventor?

The world is really blessed with many great inventors. Giving an undisputable answer to this question is therefore almost impossible. Nevertheless, a few of them deserve the accolade; one of which is Thomas Alva Edison. Thomas Edison was an American inventor and businessman with 1093 patents to his name. 1093? Wow. That is just…Wow!

He contributed to the incandescent light bulb, phonograph, electrical systems, motion picture camera, telegraph, telephone, X-ray…you seriously shouldn’t expect me to list a thousand things here. To know his inventions and contributions, you only have to look around you…read more

entry.title: Exposition to Radio Broadcasting - A Visit to Diamond FM.

link.title: Exposition to Radio Broadcasting - A Visit to Diamond FM.

entry.content: To familiarize with the process and operation of radio broadcasting, 300 level students of the department recently made a visit to Diamond FM, Ibadan. This was necessary to supplement their theoretical training of Communication Systems where they have been introduced to the basic components of a communication system: the transmitter, channel and the receiver.The students were accompanied by their lecturer, Mr. Olufajo, who is also responsible for organizing the educative exercise. Mr Tony, the station manager, was on ground to receive the students. In his welcome address, he gave a brief history of the station. According to him, the station was established in 2008 to commemorate the 60 years anniversary of the university. The idea, as initially conceived by the Faculty of Agriculture of the institution, was to create a farm radio where instant information on planting, technological advancement in agriculture and market prospects could be passed across to farmers to boost productivity. This was received in a good light by the school management and the idea was further extended to serve the entire university community…read more
entry.summary: SEEES UI Visit to Diamond FM

entry.title: SEEES UI organizes Automation and Control Training

link.title: SEEES UI organizes Automation and Control Training

entry.content: Technology is changing every day; I am pretty sure you are aware of this. But did you also know that the pace of technological advancement is such that at every twelve to eighteen months, computers double their capabilities? According to a publication on the Implications of Historical Trends in the Electrical Efficiency of Computing by Jonathan Koomey-a professor at Stanford University-the electrical efficiency of computation has doubled roughly every year and a half for more than six decades. To therefore tread these dynamic but intriguing terrains of technology, it is pertinent for us as engineers to brace ourselves with the latest advancement in world technology…read more

entry.title: JAW WAR 2016 - Stand up! Speak up! Shut up!

link.title: JAW WAR 2016 - Stand up! Speak up! Shut up!

entry.content: The stage is electrifying; the audience is intrigued; swords of words are screeching in the arena. Jaw War is here again! Jaw War, the biggest and most anticipated public speaking and oratory contest in the University of Ibadan set sail on Wednesday, 14th of September, 2016, with the first round of debates for the Halls of Residence. The theme for this year’s contest is Nigeria;Africa’s heartbeat in Coma. The defending champion and one of the favourites of the contest-the Faculty of Social Sciences- was pitched with the Faculty of Technology in the second phase of the debate, in the clash of the Faculties. The topic for discuss was Bicameral or Unicameral: The best system of legislation? Faculty of Technology spoke in support of the Unicameral system…read more

entry.title: SEEES Picnic 2016 - Eating, Games and More Fun.

link.title: SEEES Picnic 2016 - Eating, Games and More Fun.

entry.content:
Aug 27th 2016 would remain a memorable day for many SEEESites. This day, students of Electrical/Electronic Engineering, University of Ibadan, under the aegis of Society of Electrical/Electronic Engineering organized a picnic; a fun filled one in all ramifications. After a hectic first semester, the picnic was necessary to revitalize the students and usher in the new semester. It also served as a means of attenuating the fears and anxiety of awaiting the results of the preceding semester. The event was open to students from other departments to attend.

The rain was trickling with cold breeze at Awba Dam on that cool Saturday afternoon, but the students had their plans: nothing would stop them from catching fun that day. They defied the trickling rain and arrived in their numbers…read more
